core_content.markdown:
where 

$$\begin{aligned}C_{j}=\#\{i<j:\mathrm{sgn}(a_{i})=\mathrm{sgn}(a_{j})\}\\-\#\{i<j:\mathrm{sgn}(a_{i})\neq\mathrm{sgn}(a_{j})\}.\end{aligned}$$

Consider the partial sum $P_{k}=\textstyle\sum_{j=1}^{k}C_{j}$ .If exactly $p_{k}$ of $a_{1},\ldots,a_{k}$ are positive, then this sum is equal to 

$$\binom{p_k}{2}+\binom{k-p_k}{2}-\left[\binom{k}{2}-\binom{p_k}{2}-\binom{k-p_k}{2}\right],$$

which expands and simplifies to 

$$-2p_{k}(k-p_{k})+{\binom{k}{2}}.$$

For $k\leq2p$ even, this partial sum would be minimized with $p_{k}=\frac{k}{2}$ , and would then equal $\textstyle-{\frac{k}{2}};{\mathrm{f o r}}k<2p$ , odd,this partial sum would be minimized with $\begin{array}{r}{p_{k}=\frac{k\pm1}{2}}\end{array}$ ,and would then equal $-\frac{k-1}{2}$ . Either way,$\textstyle P_{k}\geq-\lfloor{\frac{k}{2}}\rfloor$ . On the other hand,$\mathrm{i f}\;k>2p,$ ,then 



$$-2p_{k}(k-p_{k})+{k\choose2}\geq-2p(k-p)+{k\choose2}$$

since $p_{k}$ is at most p. Define $Q_{k}$ to be $-\lfloor{\textstyle{\frac{k}{2}}}\rfloor{\mathrm{~i f~}}k\leq2p$ and $-2p(k-p)+\binom{k}{2}$ if $k\geq2p$ ,so that $P_{k}\geq Q_{k}$ . Note that $Q_{1}=0$ 



Partial summation gives 

$$\begin{align*}\sum_{j=1}^{n}r_{j}C_{j}&=r_{n}P_{n}+\sum_{j=2}^{n}(r_{j-1}-r_{j})P_{j-1}\\&\geq r_{n}Q_{n}+\sum_{j=2}^{n}(r_{j-1}-r_{j})Q_{j-1}\\&=\sum_{j=2}^{n}r_{j}(Q_{j}-Q_{j-1})\\&=-r_{2}-r_{4}-\cdots-r_{2p}+\sum_{j=2p+1}^{n}(j-1-2p)r_{j}.\end{align*}$$

It follows that 

$$\begin{aligned}\sum_{1\leq i<j\leq n}|a_i+a_j|&=\sum_{i=1}^{n}(n-i)r_i+\sum_{j=1}^{n}r_j C_j\\&\geq\sum_{i=1}^{2p}(n-i-[i even])r_i\\&\quad+\sum_{i=2p+1}^{n}(n-1-2p)r_i\\&=(n-1-2p)\sum_{i=1}^{n}r_i\\&\quad+\sum_{i=1}^{2p}(2p+1-i-[i even])r_i\\&\geq(n-1-2p)\sum_{i=1}^{n}r_i+p\sum_{i=1}^{2p}r_i\\&\geq(n-1-2p)\sum_{i=1}^{n}r_i+p\frac{2p}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{n}r_i,\end{aligned}$$

as desired. The next-to-last and last inequalities each follow from the monotonicity of the ${r_{i}}^{\prime}\mathrm{s}$ , the former by pairing the $i^{\mathrm{t h}}$ term with the $(2p+1-i)^{\mathrm{t h}}$ 

Note: Compare the closely related Problem 6 from the 2000 USA Mathematical Olympiad: prove that for any nonnegative real numbers $\overbrace{a_{1},\ldots,a_{n}},b_{1},\ldots,b_{n}$ , one has 

$$\sum_{i,j=1}^{n}\min\{a_{i}a_{j},b_{i}b_{j}\}\leq\sum_{i,j=1}^{n}\min\{a_{i}b_{j},a_{j}b_{i}\}.$$
